title: Models
titleUz: Namunalar — role model va peer group

intro.uz: Models — namunalar: role model (ibrat oladigan inson) va peer group (atrofingizdagi muhit). AJ zoʻr oʻrganuvchilardan oʻrganib, ularning eng yaxshi usullarini “oʻgʻirlashni” oʻrgatadi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi — solishtirish va taʼriflash tili: qiyosiy va orttirma daraja hamda (not) as … as; va aniqlovchi ergash gap (who / that / which / whose).
intro.en: Models — the people you learn from: role models (heroes you want to imitate) and your peer group (the people around you). AJ teaches you to study top learners and copy their best methods. This week's two grammar topics are the language of comparing and defining: comparatives, superlatives & (not) as … as, and defining relative clauses (who / that / which / whose).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻz namunangizni (“a person who…”) taʼriflab bera olasiz va oʻzingizni u bilan toʻxtamasdan solishtira olasiz (better than…, the best…, (not) as … as…).
canDo.en: By the end you can describe your role model (“a person who…”) and compare yourself to them without pausing (better than…, the best…, (not) as … as…).

grammar.titleUz: Qiyosiy va orttirma daraja; (not) as … as
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — compare people, places and things
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can compare things using -er / more, the -est / most and (not) as … as
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada sifatga -roq qoʻshiladi (“tezroq”) va “…dan” bilan solishtiriladi; “eng” esa orttirma daraja beradi; oʻxshatish “-dek / kabi” bilan. Ingliz tilida: qiyosiy = -er/more + than, orttirma = the -est/most, tenglik = as … as. “…dan” oʻrnida doim than turadi, orttirmada esa deyarli doim the keladi.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ She is more faster than me. She is the most fast. ✅ She is faster than me. She is the fastest. Qisqa sifatga faqat -er/-est qoʻshiladi (more/most bilan birga emas). as … as orasida esa sof sifat: as fast as (❗ as faster as emas).

examples.en: She wanted to be the best tri-athlete in the world.
examples.uz: U dunyodagi eng zoʻr triatlonchi boʻlishni istardi.

examples.en: He speaks English as fluently as a native.
examples.uz: U ingliz tilida ona tili egasidek ravon gapiradi.

exercises.prompt: Jeannie learned much ___ than the other students.
exercises.hintUz: ikki tomonni solishtirish → qiyosiy daraja + than: faster than

exercises.prompt: Tiger Woods is one of the ___ golfers in history.
exercises.hintUz: eng zoʻri (orttirma daraja) → the best; good → better → best

exercises.prompt: My English isn't ___ good ___ hers yet.
exercises.hintUz: tenglik/tengsizlik qolipi: (not) as + sof sifat + as

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingizni namunangiz bilan solishtirib uchta jumla ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “My role model is … than me. I'm not as … as … yet. One day I will be the …”.

grammar.titleUz: Aniqlovchi ergash gap: who / that / which / whose
grammar.titleEn: Defining relative clauses (who / that / which / whose)
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — define and describe people precisely
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can define a noun with who / that / which / whose
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida aniqlovchi otdan OLDIN keladi va feʼl sifatdosh boʻladi: “meni oʻqitgan oʻqituvchi”. Ingliz tili teskari quradi — avval ot, keyin who/that/which bilan izoh: “the teacher who taught me”. Odam uchun who, narsa uchun which, ikkalasi uchun that, egalik (“…ning”) uchun whose.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ The teacher which taught me. A person which does three sports. ✅ The teacher who taught me. A person who does three sports. Odam uchun who/that; narsa uchun which/that; egalik uchun whose (odamga which qoʻllanmaydi).

examples.en: A role model is someone who has already succeeded.
examples.uz: Role model — bu allaqachon muvaffaqiyatga erishgan inson.

examples.en: A tri-athlete is a person who does three sports.
examples.uz: Triatlonchi — uch xil sport bilan shugʻullanadigan inson.

examples.en: This is the method that works best.
examples.uz: Bu — eng yaxshi natija beradigan usul.

exercises.prompt: A role model is a person ___ has already done what you want to do.
Effortless English is a method ___ focuses on listening.
exercises.hintUz: narsa uchun → that yoki which (who emas)

exercises.prompt: Steve Kaufmann is a linguist ___ English is perfect.

exercises.promptUz: Namunangizni taʼriflab ikkita jumla ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “My role model is a person who … . I want a peer group that …”.
exercises.hintUz: odam → who/that; narsa → that/which; egalik → whose

vocab.uz: namuna, ibrat oladigan inson (muayyan sohada qahramon)
vocab.defEn: someone you want to imitate, usually in one specific part of life
vocab.example: Michael Jordan is my role model for basketball.

vocab.uz: tez rivojlanish yoʻlida, jadal oʻsish yoʻlida
vocab.defEn: in a situation where you improve or advance very quickly
vocab.example: Jane is on the fast track — she is learning very fast.

vocab.defEn: by chance, without a plan or clear reason
vocab.example: In the beginning I randomly chose exercises from a book.

vocab.defEn: to return something to a higher level
vocab.example: Your peer group will bring your energy back up.

vocab.defEn: an equal; someone you have regular contact with
vocab.example: Your coworkers and friends are your peers.

vocab.uz: muhit, tengdoshlar davrasi (har kuni muloqotda boʻladigan odamlar)
vocab.defEn: the group of people you associate with every day
vocab.example: You need a strong, positive peer group.

vocab.uz: yulduz; zoʻr usta, aʼlo darajadagi kishi
vocab.defEn: a very famous person; also a true master or excellent performer
vocab.example: The superstar learners all do the same things.

vocab.defEn: a person who studies or learns many languages
vocab.example: Steve Kaufmann is a linguist who speaks twelve languages.

vocab.defEn: learning something through a lot of repetition until it is automatic
vocab.example: Superstar students do a lot of deep learning.

vocab.defEn: knowing something completely, not just at a shallow level
vocab.example: You need total mastery of the basics, not just to know them.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda ikki kishi odamlarning yuzini tasvirlab, kim ekanini topish oʻyinini oʻynaydi (uzun burun, quyuq qoshlar, xol, sepkil…). Models darsida biz kimgadir tashqi koʻrinishiga emas, natijalari va usullariga qarab ergashamiz. Sizningcha, odamni faqat yuziga qarab toʻgʻri baholab boʻladimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue two people play a guessing game, describing faces (a roman nose, bushy eyebrows, a mole, freckles…). The Models lesson says we should follow people for their results and methods, not their looks. Do you think you can judge someone correctly just from their face?

keyVocab.defEn: a small natural hollow in the flesh, especially in the cheek
a small dark brown spot on a person's skin
small brownish spots on someone's skin
a face that has the shape of an egg
eyes that are set deep in the face
having little or no hair on the head

quiz.q: How long does Professor Todorov say it takes to form an impression about someone's face?
quiz.qUz: Professor Todorovning aytishicha, odamning yuzi haqida taassurot hosil qilish qancha vaqt oladi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: a) Under one second — bir soniyadan ham kam. Rob b) one second dedi va bir oz xato qildi. Todorovning tadqiqotiga koʻra, biz yuzga qarab bir soniyadan ham tezroq baho beramiz — lekin bu baho koʻpincha notoʻgʻri boʻladi.

vocab.defEn: the opinion you form about someone after meeting them only briefly
able to be trusted; worth people's trust
energetic and enjoying spending time with other people
a quality or feature of a person or thing

vocab.uz: idrok qilmoq, qanday koʻrinsa shunday qabul qilmoq
vocab.defEn: to see or understand someone or something in a particular way

vocab.uz: ich-ichidan sezib qaror qilmoq (mantiqqa emas, hissiyotga tayanmoq)
vocab.defEn: to make a decision based on instinct rather than rational thought

funEnglish.title: Who inspires you? Why heroes, role models, and mentors matter | Dyan deNapoli | TEDxDrewMiddleSchool

speakingPrompt.en: Talk about a role model — a person who inspires you. Who are they, and what do they do that you admire? How are they better than most people, and are you as good as them yet?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: qiyosiy va orttirma daraja ((not) as … as; better than; the best) hamda aniqlovchi ergash gap (a person who…; a method that…).
